name: Inés María Chapman Waugh
position: Deputy Prime Minister of the Republic of Cuba
body: Innovative workers who ensure the functioning of weather radars and early warning systems.

name: Elba Rosa Pérez Montoya
position: Minister of Science, Technology and Environment
body: Progress is being made in the sustainability of the country's Weather Radar Network; we will achieve a 100% Cuban radar, the fruit of the talent and professionalism of the scientists and workers of the Camagüey Radar Group.

name: José Fidel Santana Nuñez
position: First Deputy Minister of Science, Technology and Environment
body: A team that demonstrates that genius and creativity are everywhere, and that being 'National' is not exclusive to capitals. One day we will do justice to this team and honor ourselves with the tribute they have long deserved.

name: Armando Rodríguez
position: Deputy Minister of Science, Technology and Environment
body: A contribution of Cuban science, technology, and innovation to the Environment: the automation and Dopplerization of Cuban radars!

name: Enrique Castellanos Abella
position: Director of Geology, Ministry of Energy and Mines
body: Cuba has a meteorological radar system to research and monitor cyclones and hurricanes in real time. This infrastructure is maintained by Cuban specialists.

name: Yasser Llanes Jorge
body: I have worked with this team for years and their knowledge, service, and human quality are impeccable.

name: Dario Jorge Vázquez Arguija
body: Working at LADETEC was an extraordinary opportunity for my professional career; I ventured into industrial automation and geolocation. They instilled pragmatism in me and taught me how to focus effort on concrete results.
